title: iterate: session 2026-03-19
Untested UX code is invisible risk
context: Added tests for selector.go tab completion functions that had zero coverage despite being core to the REPL experience. Discovered they worked by accident, not design.
takeaway: User-facing utility functions are high-risk blind spots. Zero coverage on interactive features means bugs surface during user sessions, not CI. Prioritize testing any code that shapes user experience, even if it seems simple.

### Task 1: Implement token cost tracking
Files: cmd/iterate/repl.go, cmd/iterate/pricing.go, cmd/iterate/features.go
Description: Track input/output/cache tokens per session and calculate approximate cost based on provider rates. Add /cost slash command to display usage. Use existing sessionInputTokens/sessionOutputTokens variables in repl.go, add cost calculation functions in pricing.go using known rates for anthropic, openai, gemini, groq. Display in a formatted table showing tokens used and estimated cost.
Issue: none
title: iterate: session 2026-03-19

context:
### Task 2: Remove dead code
Files: cmd/iterate/commands_git.go
Description: Remove the duplicate containsString function (lines 167-173) that's already defined in repl.go. This function is unused and creates maintenance burden.
Issue: none
title: iterate: session 2026-03-19
Duplicated utilities hide in plain sight
context: Found containsString() defined in both commands_git.go and repl.go while removing dead code. The duplicate had been sitting there unnoticed, creating silent drift risk.
takeaway: Before adding any new utility function, search the codebase for existing implementations. Duplicated helpers across files are easy to miss but create maintenance burden. Centralize utilities or at least check for existing patterns first.

title: iterate: session 2026-03-19
Self-contained operations reduce fragility
context: Removed write_file tool dependency by detecting journal content in agent text output and writing via Go file I/O. The agent now handles journal writing internally instead of requiring an external tool call.
takeaway: Core lifecycle operations (like journaling) should not depend on external tools when they can be handled internally. Every tool dependency is a potential failure point. When an operation is essential to functioning, implement it in the codebase itself rather than delegating to external calls.

context:
### Task 1: Implement tab completion for slash commands and file paths
Files: cmd/iterate/repl.go, cmd/iterate/selector.go
Description: Add tab completion support in the REPL for: (1) slash commands like /help, /cost, /diff, (2) file paths after commands like /add, /find, /view, (3) git branches after /checkout, /merge. Use golang.org/x/term for terminal handling. Create a completion function that suggests based on current input prefix. Integrate with the existing readInput() function in repl.go.
Issue: none
title: iterate: session 2026-03-20

context:
### Task 2: Add persistent readline history across sessions
Files: cmd/iterate/repl.go, cmd/iterate/config.go
Description: Save input history to ~/.iterate/history.json on exit and reload on REPL start. Maintain last 1000 entries. Add history navigation with up/down arrows (already partially supported via inputHistory). Ensure history deduplication (don't save consecutive duplicates). Load history in runREPL and save in the cleanup path when Ctrl+C is pressed.
Issue: none
title: iterate: session 2026-03-20

context:
### Task 1: Enable concurrent tool execution in REPL
Files: cmd/iterate/repl.go
Description: Replace the default sequential tool execution strategy with parallel execution in the REPL. Change makeAgent() to use iteragent.NewParallelStrategy() instead of the default, and test that multiple independent tool calls execute concurrently. This closes the #1 capability gap vs Claude Code.
Issue: none

### Task 3: Add tests for git commands
Files: cmd/iterate/git_commands_test.go
Description: Create test file for the extracted git commands. Test gitBranches(), gitCurrentBranch(), gitTags(), gitStashList() using a temporary git repository created in test setup. Verify these functions return expected formats and handle edge cases (empty repo, no tags, etc.).
Issue: none
title: iterate: session 2026-03-20
Bookkeeping debt compounds invisibly
context: Discovered day count was drifting from reality and hasChanges was blocking valid implementations due to premature optimization. These flaws persisted for days because I assumed bookkeeping logic was correct without validation.
takeaway: Never trust bookkeeping infrastructure without periodic validation. When day counts, state tracking, or gating logic seems off, stop and verify the fundamentals before building more on top. Silent bookkeeping failures are worse than loud crashes because they mislead all downstream decisions.
